meta.name: Netherlands
meta.aboutStudying: The Netherlands is one of Europe's most popular study destinations, with over 2,100 English-taught programmes — the highest outside the UK. Fees are low for EU students (€2,314/yr statutory fee) and universities consistently rank in the world top 200. Dutch cities are safe, student-friendly and internationally connected.

tips.title: Get a Bike Immediately
tips.text: The Netherlands is the most cycling-friendly country on earth. A second-hand bike costs €50–150 and is essential for student life. Public transport is excellent but expensive — an OV-chipkaart student subscription saves you 40% on trains.

tips.title: EU Tuition Fees
tips.text: EU/EEA students pay the same statutory fee as Dutch students — around €2,314/year. Non-EU students pay €6,000–20,000/year depending on the programme. DUO student finance (interest-free loans and grants) is available for EU students working part-time in the Netherlands.

tips.title: Everyone Speaks English
tips.text: The Dutch have the highest English proficiency of any non-native speaking country in the world. Over 2,100 full degree programmes are taught entirely in English. Learning some Dutch will earn you tremendous goodwill but is never strictly necessary.

universities.name: University of Amsterdam
universities.desc: The Netherlands' largest research university, ranked in the QS world top 60, with a vibrant campus in the heart of Amsterdam.

universities.name: Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam
universities.desc: A broad research university known for its independent spirit, strong medical faculty and excellent Amsterdam campus.

universities.name: Delft University of Technology
universities.desc: Europe's top technical university for engineering and architecture — ranked #13 globally in QS Engineering.

universities.name: Utrecht University
universities.desc: The Netherlands' highest-ranked university overall (QS top 100), known for outstanding research and a beautiful historic campus.

universities.name: Erasmus University Rotterdam
universities.desc: Home to the world-renowned Rotterdam School of Management, consistently ranked among Europe's top business schools.

universities.name: University of Groningen
universities.desc: A top-100 global university in a vibrant, affordable student city — among the top destinations for international students in the Netherlands.

universities.name: Leiden University
universities.desc: The oldest Dutch university, home to 16 Nobel Prize winners and a highly respected law faculty with centuries of tradition.

universities.name: Eindhoven University of Technology
universities.desc: Consistently ranked among Europe's top technical universities, closely linked to Philips and the high-tech Brainport region.

universities.name: Radboud University
universities.desc: A broad research university in one of the Netherlands' oldest cities, with a particularly strong medical faculty and Green Campus.

universities.name: Maastricht University
universities.desc: Europe's most international university by student population (55% international), known for problem-based learning and a cozy student city.

universities.name: Tilburg University
universities.desc: A specialist social sciences university with one of the Netherlands' top economics and law programmes.

universities.name: Wageningen University
universities.desc: World #1 for agriculture and forestry sciences — a unique specialist university attracting students from 130+ countries.

universities.name: Erasmus MC – Medical Centre
universities.desc: The Netherlands' largest academic medical centre and Europe's leading university medical centre for research.

universities.name: University of Twente
universities.desc: An entrepreneurial technical university set on a beautiful green campus, known for start-up culture and engineering innovation.

universities.name: Open University Netherlands
universities.desc: Distance learning university offering flexible degree programmes — ideal for working professionals and mature students.

universities.name: HAN University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: One of the Netherlands' largest applied sciences universities, offering practical programmes with strong industry connections.

universities.name: Saxion University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A major applied sciences university with three campuses across eastern Netherlands, known for international student community.

universities.name: HZ University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A small coastal applied sciences university specialising in water, delta and harbour management programmes.

universities.name: The Hague University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: Located in the international city of peace and justice, with strong programmes in international law and public administration.

universities.name: Inholland University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A broad applied sciences university with campuses across the Netherlands, known for creative and applied programmes.

universities.name: Avans University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: One of the largest applied sciences universities in the Netherlands with a strong focus on entrepreneurship and innovation.

universities.name: Fontys University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: One of the Netherlands' largest applied sciences universities with a diverse range of programmes across multiple campuses.

universities.name: HKU University of the Arts Utrecht
universities.desc: The Netherlands' leading arts university with outstanding programmes in fine arts, design, music and digital media.

universities.name: Rotterdam University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: The largest applied sciences university in the Netherlands, with one of Europe's most diverse student populations.

universities.name: Amsterdam University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: The second-largest higher education institution in Amsterdam, offering practical programmes with a strong city focus.

universities.name: Zuyd University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A cross-border university in southern Netherlands, with strong connections to Belgian and German universities and industries.

universities.name: Hanze University of Applied Sciences Groningen
universities.desc: The largest applied sciences university in northern Netherlands, with over 70 English-taught programmes.

universities.name: Utrecht University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: One of the Netherlands' largest applied sciences universities, with a strong focus on social innovation.

universities.name: Windesheim University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A Christian-inspired broad applied sciences university in central Netherlands with internationally oriented programmes.

universities.name: NHL Stenden University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A modern applied sciences university formed from two merged institutions, with a strong focus on hospitality and tourism.

universities.name: Breda University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A specialist applied sciences university focused on hospitality, tourism, logistics and digital media.

universities.name: University of Amsterdam – Economics
universities.desc: The Amsterdam School of Economics, part of UvA — consistently ranked among Europe's top economics faculties.

universities.name: Erasmus School of Law
universities.desc: One of Europe's leading law schools, consistently ranked among the top 50 globally for law.

universities.name: Rotterdam School of Management
universities.desc: Consistently ranked in the top 10 European business schools by the Financial Times — a truly global management school.

universities.name: Design Academy Eindhoven
universities.desc: One of the world's most influential design schools — consistently at the vanguard of contemporary design thinking.

universities.name: Rotterdam Conservatory
universities.desc: The Netherlands' leading conservatory for music, dance and circus arts, with world-class performance facilities.

universities.name: Conservatorium van Amsterdam
universities.desc: One of the world's leading music conservatories, situated on Amsterdam's vibrant Oosterdok waterfront.

universities.name: Gerrit Rietveld Academie
universities.desc: Amsterdam's premier fine arts and design academy, named after the De Stijl architect and influential to this day.

universities.name: Royal Academy of Art The Hague
universities.desc: One of Europe's oldest art academies, offering outstanding fine arts, graphic design and interior architecture programmes.

universities.name: ArtEZ University of the Arts
universities.desc: A multi-campus arts university spanning fashion, fine arts, music, theatre and design across three Dutch cities.

universities.name: NHTV Breda – Tourism Academy
universities.desc: A specialist hospitality, tourism and leisure university with a strong international reputation in the sector.

universities.name: The Hague Academy of International Law
universities.desc: The world's leading institution for the study of international law, attracting academics and practitioners from across the globe.

universities.name: Nyenrode Business University
universities.desc: The Netherlands' only private business university, set on a historic estate, known for leadership and entrepreneurship programmes.

universities.name: Leiden University – Psychology Faculty
universities.desc: The Netherlands' top psychology faculty, part of the historic Leiden University with outstanding research output.

universities.name: University Medical Centre Groningen
universities.desc: One of the largest academic medical centres in Europe, affiliated with the University of Groningen.

universities.name: Leiden University Medical Centre
universities.desc: One of the world's leading academic medical centres, affiliated with the oldest Dutch university.

universities.name: University of Twente – Management Faculty
universities.desc: An entrepreneurial management faculty in the heart of a technical university, known for high-tech business programmes.

universities.name: TU Delft – Architecture Faculty
universities.desc: Consistently ranked as the world's #1 or #2 architecture school — a global destination for aspiring architects.

universities.name: Florcitura University of Applied Sciences
universities.desc: A small, focused applied sciences institution in Amsterdam with specialist creative and business programmes.

universities.name: Christelijke Hogeschool Ede
universities.desc: A Christian-oriented applied sciences university in central Netherlands with community-focused programmes.

Amsterdam.desc: The canal city — 165 nationalities, world-class museums, vibrant nightlife and more English-language university programmes than any mainland European city.
Amsterdam.studentLife: Exceptional — active student unions, international community, easy cycling everywhere.
Amsterdam.transport: Excellent tram, metro and ferry network; NS trains to all Dutch cities.
Amsterdam.nightlife: World-famous clubs, jazz bars, brown cafés and rooftop terraces.

Rotterdam.desc: Europe's largest port city has reinvented itself as a bold architectural capital — cheaper, edgier and more entrepreneurial than Amsterdam.
Rotterdam.studentLife: Dynamic — strong focus on entrepreneurship and design; very international.
Rotterdam.transport: Metro, tram and water taxi; 30 min from Amsterdam by Intercity.
Rotterdam.nightlife: Thriving club scene around the Witte de Withstraat and waterfront venues.

Utrecht.desc: The Netherlands' most student-friendly city — a medieval centre, low-level canals and the country's highest-ranked university all in one compact package.
Utrecht.studentLife: Outstanding — one-third of the city is a student, massive social scene.
Utrecht.transport: Central Station is the busiest in the Netherlands; 25 min from Amsterdam.
Utrecht.nightlife: Compact but excellent — numerous student bars, live music and canal-side terraces.

Delft.desc: A picture-perfect Dutch canal city dominated by TU Delft — the world's top engineering and architecture school in one of Europe's most photogenic settings.
Delft.studentLife: Intimate student community centred around the university campus.
Delft.transport: 10 min by tram to Rotterdam; direct trains to The Hague and Amsterdam.
Delft.nightlife: Small but lively student pub scene; easy access to Rotterdam's clubs.

Groningen.desc: The student capital of northern Netherlands — the highest proportion of students per capita in the country and one of Europe's most affordable university cities.
Groningen.studentLife: Unparalleled — a quarter of the population are students; incredible social scene.
Groningen.transport: Good regional connections; direct trains to Amsterdam (2 hrs).
Groningen.nightlife: Legendary Dutch student nightlife — Poelestraat known as the party street.

Leiden.desc: The oldest Dutch university city — a compact, beautifully preserved canal city with world-class academic heritage and just 20 minutes from both Amsterdam and The Hague.
highlights: Pieterskerk and the Pilgrim Fathers connection
Leiden.studentLife: Strong academic community with numerous student associations.
Leiden.transport: Intercity trains every 15 minutes to Amsterdam, Rotterdam, The Hague.
Leiden.nightlife: Pleasant student bar scene; easy access to bigger cities.

cityCards.desc: 165 nationalities, canal rings, world-class museums and the most English-taught programmes in mainland Europe.
Europe's boldest city of architecture, Erasmus University and Europe's largest port.
The most student-friendly city in the Netherlands — compact, affordable and home to the country's highest-ranked university.
The world's #1 engineering and architecture university in a picture-perfect canal city.
Student capital of the north — the highest student density in the Netherlands with legendary nightlife.
The oldest Dutch university city — historic canals, Nobel laureates and 20 minutes from Amsterdam.
